test.notes: Full suite exited with only the 2 known pre-existing failures documented in AGENTS.md: aiOrchestratorService single-lane integration closing strategy and orchestratorService unified-attempt laneId handling. No additional failures were observed (1150 passed, 2 failed).

lint.notes: Lint is not configured in the project manifest.

blockingIssues.description: VAL-UX-010 remains unmet: the sidebar resize implementation uses percentage bounds instead of the required 200-400px range and never persists width updates back to localStorage.

VAL-UX-002 remains unmet: the rendered activity feed still groups with legacy `groupConsecutive()` logic, while the new `collapseFeedMessages()` helper is only covered by tests and is not wired into runtime UI rendering.
VAL-USAGE-004 and VAL-USAGE-005 remain unmet: reset countdowns are hidden in tooltips rather than shown inline, and mission cost is derived from global provider totals with no per-worker breakdown.
VAL-UX-006 and VAL-UX-009 remain unmet: the header ARCHIVE action only cleans lanes instead of archiving the mission, and multiple mission UI files still ship font sizes below 10px.
VAL-USAGE-003 remains unmet: `modelDowngradeThresholdPct` is only wired into UI/types and is never consumed by orchestrator runtime to downgrade newly spawned workers or emit feed events.
VAL-PLAN-007 remains unmet: phase transitions ignore `requiresApproval`, and no approval intervention is created when leaving a phase marked for manual approval.
VAL-USAGE-002 remains incomplete: Mission Settings still does not expose the promised budget-cap controls because `MissionSettingsDialog` never mounts `SmartBudgetPanel`.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented the local `SplitPane.tsx` wrapper as the preferred resizable mission layout pattern for UI workers using `react-resizable-panels`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require UI workers to explicitly record missing agent-browser verification and required library reads as procedure deviations instead of marking `followedProcedure: true` when they skipped those mandated steps.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `ui-overhaul-layout-interventions-usage` review found that the worker skipped `.factory/library/user-testing.md` and agent-browser validation even though the ui-worker skill requires both, yet the handoff still reported `followedProcedure: true`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Avoid assigning renderer-only ui-worker features to contracts that also require orchestrator runtime enforcement; split those assertions into a service-worker feature or explicitly flag the cross-layer scope up front.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `budget-planning-approval-ui` review found that the feature added UI/type plumbing but missed runtime requirements in `missionBudgetService.ts`, `coordinatorTools.ts`, and `orchestratorService.ts`, matching the narrower renderer-oriented ui-worker skill guidance.
